channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Kuwait, Bahrain respond to Iran attacks
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kuwait and Bahrain’s militaries confirm they are responding to Iranian aerial attacks, with air raid sirens sounding across both Gulf nations. Read more on
Read More: https://punchng.com/kuwait-bahrain-respond-to-iran-attacks/

item.title: 50 Malian soldiers killed in rebel-jihadist attack — Officials
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
At least 50 Malian soldiers were killed in a rebel-jihadist attack near Anefis, officials confirm. This Mali attack is the deadliest in years.
Read More: https://punchng.com/50-malian-soldiers-killed-in-rebel-jihadist-attack-officials/

item.title: Scorecard: England vs India, third ODI, Lord's
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Latest from the series-deciding third one-day international between England and India. Watch live on Sky Sports Cricket.

item.title: Two firefighters hospitalised as fire guts Lagos three-storey building
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Two firefighters were hospitalised after battling a Lagos building fire in Ketu-Ikosi. An electrical surge caused the blaze in the three-storey residential
Read More: https://punchng.com/two-firefighters-hospitalised-as-fire-guts-lagos-three-storey-building/

item.title: An opportunity missed? | England World Cup Verdict
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Sky Sports football correspondent Rob Dorsett has been with England every step of the way at the World Cup - he reflects on England's performance at the tournament and what could have been.

item.title: Withdraw social media bill or face lawsuit, SERAP tells NASS
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
SERAP warns NASS to withdraw the proposed Social Media Bill 2026, calling it a backdoor attempt to control online expression, or face legal action.
Read More: https://punchng.com/withdraw-social-media-bill-or-face-lawsuit-serap-tells-nass/

item.title: England bat first vs India in ODI decider as Bumrah misses out injured LIVE!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Christian Women Urge Tinubu To Reject Bill Banning Preaching In Commercial Buses
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Women Wing of the Christian Association of Nigeria (WOWICAN) has called on President Bola Ahmed Tinubu to withhold assent to any provision of the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) Act (Amendment) Bill, 2026, that seeks to criminalise peaceful preaching in commercial buses. In a statement made ava

item.title: 2027: Persons with disabilities in Ogun vow unity, INEC partnership

item.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

item.title: NDLEA Intercepts Over 1.5 million Tramadol Pills Concealed In Trucks From Benin Republic
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Operatives of the National Drug Law Enforcement have intercepted more than 1.5 million pills of Tramadol concealed in two trucks that entered Nigeria through the Benin Republic border, dealing another major blow to drug trafficking syndicates. The latest seizure comes barely a week after the agency&

item.title: FG warns of fake ₦50,000 allowance registration scam

item.title: BIG F2 crash sparks fire forcing red-flag stoppage in Belgium
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: A big crash saw Trident driver Laurens van Hoepen taken out during the F2 race at the Belgian Grand Prix.

item.title: FG debunks ₦50,000 National Support Allowance registration claims
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has dismissed reports claiming that registration has begun for a ₦50,000 National Support Allowance, describing the information as false and warning Nigerians against falling victim to online fraudsters. The post FG debunks ₦50,000 National Support Allowance registration claim

item.title: Troops foil mass abduction attempt at FGGC Monguno
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The FGGC facility is currently being utilized by the Borno State Government as temporary hostel accommodation for students of the Federal Polytechnic, Monguno town. The post Troops foil mass abduction attempt at FGGC Monguno appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: The stage is set for a blockbuster as the 2026 FIFA World Cup comes to an end, with Spain and Argentina preparing to battle for football's biggest prize in the world. This is a clash that brings together two generations of greatness, with Lionel Messi looking to add another unforgettable chapter to

item.description: Sunday's showpiece is likely to be the final World Cup appearance for Messi, widely considered the greatest player of all time. The post Spain, Argentina Brace For World Cup Final appeared first on Channels Television.

item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [allAfrica] There is an old saying in journalism: when the cameras leave, the suffering remains.

item.title: Step-By-Step Guide On How To Register For Your PVC On A Weekend
item.dc:creator: YNaija
item.description: As the 2027 presidential elections draw closer, the deadline (July 26th) for registering people who need to get their PVCs draws closer. The PVC is the personal voter’s card that allows Nigerian citizens over 18 to vote for their candidate of choice in Nigerian elections. If you haven’t registered f

item.title: 'Legend' or 'regrets'? A final that will define Lamine Yamal's World Cup
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Lamine Yamal may not have had a sparkling World Cup but victory for Spain in Sunday's World Cup final would write a very different narrative.

item.title: Adebayo backs state police creation
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Social Democratic Party (SDP) in the 2027 election, Prince Adewole Adebayo, has thrown his weight behind the establishment of state police in Nigeria. The post Adebayo backs state police creation appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: CDHR Criticises US Over Continued Economic Sanctions Against Cuba
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Committee for the Defence of Human Rights (CDHR) has expressed its profound concern over the continued expansion of unilateral economic sanctions and coercive measures imposed by the Government of the United States against the Republic of Cuba. The Human Rights Group, in a statement signed by

item.title: A $3.05 Billion Bet On Nigeria’s People And Why It Must Work
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: It is easy, perhaps too easy, to spend every column cataloguing what is going wrong in this country. God knows there is enough material. The insecurity, the infrastructure deficit, and the cost of living that is still biting hard on ordinary families, none of that has disappeared. But part of honest

item.title: Messi leads Argentina against Spain in blockbuster World Cup final

item.description: Lionel Messi’s battle-hardened Argentina take on silky Spain in the World Cup final on Sunday as the first 48-team tournament in history reaches a climax in New Jersey. The South Americans are striving to become the first team since Brazil in 1962 to win back-to-back World Cups, whil

item.title: At least 50 Malian soldiers killed in rebel-jihadist attack
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: At least 50 Malian soldiers were killed and dozens more were taken prisoner in an attack by Tuareg separatists and jihadists, local sources said told AFP on Sunday.

item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria

The governor made the appeal while receiving a delegation of Nigerian students study

The flag-off was witnessed by yours truly, as emergency obstetric and newborn healthcare equipment, commodit

item.title: Fulani group raises alarm over alleged use of drones to monitor settlements

item.description: A group under the auspices of Concerned Fulani in Plateau, CFP, has raised the alarm over what it described as unauthorized usage of drones to monitor Fulani settlements in rural communities, especially in Barkin Ladi, Riyom, and Bokkos local government areas. The CFP, which raised the alarm in a st

item.title: Wildfire scorches 12,000 hectares in central Spain's Guadalajara province
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Spain is facing yet another raging wildfire as local authorities said on Sunday that a blaze north of Madrid had burned more than 12,000 hectares and forced hundreds of people to evacuate. Earlier this month one of the country's deadliest ever wildfires broke out in the south and firefighters are ye

item.title: Democratic accountability: An imperative for the new federal and state police service, By Olumide Fred’ Adetiba
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: As Nigeria undertakes this historic reform of its policing architecture, we must resist the temptation to focus only on decentralising power. Equally important is democratising accountability. A decentralised police system without democratic oversight merely relocates power; it does not necessarily

item.title: Tinubu Celebrates Senator Omilani On 90th Birthday
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has joined other eminent Nigerians to celebrate former Senator and insurance mogul, Senator Yinka Omilani, as he marks his 90th birthday. In his goodwill message to the elder statesman, President Tinubu, in a press statement issued on Sunday by his Special Adviser on Informatio

item.description: “The court didn’t say NBC cannot sanction. It only said you cannot be a judge and jury in your own case,” he said.

item.description: Jake Paul said he will not fight elite heavyweights again after suffering a broken jaw in a sixth-round knockout loss to Anthony Joshua on Dec. 19 at the Kaseya Center. Joshua, who outweighed Paul by nearly 27 pounds and held a five-inch height advantage, dropped the cruiserweight four times before

item.title: Top 5 program Every Nigerian Christian Never Misses Annually

item.title: Former Players Must Lead NFF For Nigeria To Progress – Troost-Ekong
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Super Eagles captain William Troost-Ekong has declared that Nigerian football will only move forward if ex-internationals are given genuine decision-making power within the Nigeria Football Federation, as he positions himself for a post-retirement role in the game’s administrative structures.

item.description: The survivors were found clinging to a fish trap and floating debris at sea three days after the boat sank.

item.title: Mutfwang pledges lasting peace, business-friendly environment in Plateau
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Onah reaffirmed the support of the Igbo community for the Mutfwang administration and pledged its continued cooperation in advancing the development of Plateau State. The post Mutfwang pledges lasting peace, business-friendly environment in Plateau appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: World Cup Final Preview: Messi’s last dance meets Spain’s quest for new era
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Sunday promises to be far more than a football match, as it is a clash between Europe's most organised side and South America's most ruthless champions. The post World Cup Final Preview: Messi’s last dance meets Spain’s quest for new era appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Rivers Court Ends 100-Year Land Dispute In Favour Of Obuama Community
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Rivers State High Court sitting in Port Harcourt has dismissed a land ownership suit filed by the Usokun community against the Obuama community in Degema local government area of the state, reaffirming earlier judicial decisions that recognised Obuama’s ownership of the disputed Obupiri land

item.title: Adamawa ADC Dismisses Court Claims, Says INEC Recognises Party Leadership
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) governorship candidate in Adamawa State, Engineer Omar Suleiman, has dismissed reports suggesting that court rulings and the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) do not recognise the party’s current national leadership, insisting that the elect

item.title: Facebook down in UK with users hit with ‘account unavailable’ warning
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Users are being faced with the message: "Account Temporarily Unavailable. Your account is currently unavailable due to a site issue. We expect this to be resolved shortly. Please try again in a few minutes," reported the local medium.

item.title: Calm Meets Chaos As Spain, Argentina Battle For W/Cup Crown
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Spain and Argentina prepared for Sunday’s World Cup final in New Jersey, with organisers keeping a close watch on wildfire smoke drifting over parts of the United States. The wildfire drifting to the U.S. from Canada has forced President Donald Trump to threaten to slam another round of tariff

item.title: Dangerous Enemies, Dangerous Prayers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: TEXT: 2 KINGS 2-19:23, ACTS 13-6 Instead of crying and complaining go to a powerful gathering where dangerous prayers are prayed. Uncommon favour.   YOU NEED TO PRAY DANGEROUS PRAYERS WHEN; Unbelievers are asking where is your God When your marriage is at stake. You have been tied down with sic

item.title: England beat France 6-4 to finish third
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The World Cup resumed on Saturday with the third-place playoff, where England defeated France 6–4. Bukayo Saka scored a hat-trick to help the Three Lions secure their best tournament finish since 1966. Despite the defeat, Kylian Mbappé scored twice for Les Bleus to overtake Lionel Messi as the all-t

item.title: Obong Of Calabar Executive Forum Faults WAEC Over Removal Of Efik Language
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Obong of Calabar Executive Forum, OCEF, has faulted the West African Examinations Council, WAEC, over the removal of Efik Language from its curriculum, describing the action as an assault on the Efik people. The position of the Forum was made known in a statement issued in Calabar by its Chairma

item.title: French coach Didier Deschamps retires after 14 years
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: France's World Cup campaign ended in disappointment as Didier Deschamps brought his 14-year tenure as the national team's longest-serving manager to a close. The defeat marked the end of a remarkable era, during which he led Les Bleus to major international success. Morgan Aire looks back at his dis

item.title: Shettima departs Abuja for ECOWAS summit in Freetown
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The vice president will return to Abuja at the conclusion of his engagements in the West African nation. The post Shettima departs Abuja for ECOWAS summit in Freetown appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: US: Taco Bell removes lettuce after parasite outbreak
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The American fast-food chain Taco Bell is removing lettuce from its menu at many locations following reports of explosive diarrhoea linked to a multistate outbreak. The outbreak is the largest US outbreak of cyclosporiasis, a parasitic infection spread through contaminated food or water. While no de

item.title: US Launches Strikes To ‘Punish’ Iran After Troops Killed
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: A preliminary deal aimed at ending the war has collapsed as the foes fight to break the deadlock over the Strait of Hormuz, a waterway key to the world's oil supply. The post US Launches Strikes To ‘Punish’ Iran After Troops Killed appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: First US soldiers killed in Jordan, Trump vows to shift action
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Two American service members were killed and a third is missing following an Iranian attack on a US base in Jordan. The deaths are the first from hostile fire since a truce with Iran in early April and come after a week of the heaviest fighting since the conflict began in February. Saturday also mar
